title: Retaining Benign Greeting-Only Interactions
summary: Guidance for preserving harmless greeting-only interactions in minimal form without over-redacting or inventing context.
body_markdown:
# Retaining Benign Greeting-Only Interactions

## When to Use

Use this guidance when deciding whether a greeting-only interaction is safe to keep as an example of basic conversational behavior.

This applies only to generic greetings that contain no personal, operational, sensitive, or identifying information.

## Context

A common privacy pitfall is over-redacting or discarding harmless greeting-only interactions. Removing every benign greeting can eliminate useful evidence for simple conversation handling without improving privacy protection.

The safe pattern is to retain only a minimal description of the interaction, not the raw exchange and not any inferred context.

## Operating Rule

Retain a greeting-only interaction only when it is genuinely generic and non-sensitive.

Keep the record minimal:

- Describe that the interaction was a benign greeting-handling example.
- Avoid quoting raw transcript text unless necessary and clearly harmless.
- Do not infer user identity, intent, project context, location, role, or organization.
- Do not add operational meaning that was not present in the source.
- Do not preserve unnecessary metadata or unique identifiers.

Do not retain the interaction as safe if the greeting includes or is attached to:

- Names or contact details
- Account information
- Customer, team, or project context
- Private identifiers
- Sensitive personal context
- Credentials, tokens, cookies, authorization headers, or private keys
- Operational instructions or system-specific details

## Verify

Before retaining the interaction, confirm that the source contains no:

- Personal data
- Credentials or secrets
- Customer-specific or team-specific operational details
- Sensitive topics
- Unique identifiers beyond the generic greeting itself
- Context that could make the greeting identifying or operationally meaningful

If any of these are present, do not treat the interaction as a benign greeting-only example. Redact or discard according to the applicable privacy and security rules.

## Reusable Lessons

- Generic greetings without operational, personal, or sensitive content can be retained in minimal form as benign conversation-handling examples.
- Over-redacting harmless greetings may reduce the quality of conversational behavior evidence without adding privacy protection.
- The retention decision depends on the full surrounding context, not just the greeting text.
- If a greeting is connected to names, accounts, projects, private identifiers, sensitive context, or operational instructions, it is no longer a generic benign greeting.

## Agent Use

Use this page when:

- Reviewing whether a greeting-only interaction can be retained as safe evidence.
- Curating examples of basic conversational handling.
- Deciding whether minimal retention is preferable to full discard.

Apply it by:

- Checking the source for sensitive, personal, operational, or identifying context.
- Keeping only a minimal description of the benign greeting-handling pattern.
- Avoiding invented context, inferred user details, or unnecessary transcript preservation.

Verify by:

- Confirming that the retained record contains no personal data, secrets, customer details, team-specific details, sensitive topics, or unique identifiers.
- Confirming that the source was greeting-only and did not include operational instructions.

Do not use it when:

- The interaction contains names, contact details, account references, customer context, internal project context, private identifiers, or sensitive personal information.
- The interaction includes credentials, tokens, cookies, auth headers, private keys, or other secrets.
- The interaction includes operational instructions or system-specific details.
- The surrounding context makes the greeting identifiable or sensitive.

provenance.excerpt: Over-redacting or discarding harmless greeting-only interactions can remove useful evidence for basic conversational behavior without improving privacy protection. Retain only a minimal record describing the benign greeting-handling pattern; avoid adding inferred context or unnecessary details.

risk_notes: Do not preserve raw transcripts if they contain personal, operational, sensitive, or identifying context.
A greeting that appears harmless in isolation may become sensitive when combined with surrounding metadata or conversation context.
Never retain credentials, tokens, cookies, authorization headers, private keys, or private identifiers as part of a greeting example.
